message:
perf(scheduler): lock reduction, local-first task lookup, and wake state dedup fix

Three categories of scheduler improvements:

ThreeLaneWorker (three_lane.rs):
- Extracted try_local_all_lanes() method that checks cancel, timed, and
  ready lanes under a single PriorityScheduler lock acquisition, replacing
  the previous inline block that was harder to maintain.
- Pre-compute local_deadline alongside has_runnable_work in the parking
  path's lock acquisition, eliminating a second lock round-trip that was
  previously needed just to check the next timer deadline.
- Simplified SchedulingSuggestion dispatch from match-with-two-arms to
  if/else (the enum only has two meaningful variants here).

Worker (worker.rs):
- Changed seen_io_tokens from BTreeSet<u64> to HashSet<u64>.  These
  tokens are never iterated in order; the only operations are insert and
  contains, making O(1) HashSet strictly better than O(log n) BTreeSet.
- Restructured execute() to check thread-local storage for local tasks
  BEFORE locking global state.  The previous flow was: lock state → miss
  global lookup → drop lock → check thread-local → re-lock state.  The
  new flow is: check thread-local (no lock) → lock state once.  This
  saves a full lock round-trip for the common case of local tasks.
- Fixed a wake state dedup-suppression bug: when a pinned-local waiter
  has a foreign worker ID (both in the panic path and the ready path),
  the code consumed notify() but never cleared the wake bit.  This meant
  a future valid wake for that task would be permanently suppressed by
  the dedup guard.  Now calls record.wake_state.clear() in both paths.
- Added test for the foreign-waiter wake state clearing behavior.

LocalQueue (local_queue.rs):
- Added test verifying steal_batch rejects cross-arena transfers without
  mutating either the source or destination queue.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
